member.name: T:Nomad.Core.Collections.LockFreePooledQueue`1
member.summary: Lock-free queue with object pooling to reduce GC pressure. This is a C# adaptation of boost::lockfree::queue.
member.remarks: WARNING: Node reuse via pooling makes this implementation susceptible to the ABA problem. In practice, with .NET's memory model and typical usage, the risk is low, but it exists. For mission‑critical scenarios, consider using or a versioned approach (e.g., packing pointer+version into a long).
member.typeparam: Type of elements in the queue.

member.name: P:Nomad.Core.Collections.LockFreePooledQueue`1.Count
member.summary: Gets the number of elements contained in the queue.
member.remarks: This count is approximate and may not reflect concurrent operations.

member.name: P:Nomad.Core.Collections.LockFreePooledQueue`1.IsEmpty
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the queue is empty.

member.name: M:Nomad.Core.Collections.LockFreePooledQueue`1.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: Maximum number of nodes to keep in the pool.

member.name: M:Nomad.Core.Collections.LockFreePooledQueue`1.TryAdd(`0)
member.summary: Tries to add an item to the queue.
member.param: The item to add.
member.returns: Always true (unbounded queue).

member.name: M:Nomad.Core.Collections.LockFreePooledQueue`1.TryTake(`0@)
member.summary: Tries to take an item from the queue.
member.param: When this method returns, contains the item removed, if successful.
member.returns: true if an item was removed; otherwise, false.

member.summary: Tries to enqueue an item.

member.summary: Tries to dequeue an item.

member.name: M:Nomad.Core.Collections.LockFreePooledQueue`1.Clear
member.summary: Removes all items from the queue.
member.remarks: This operation is NOT thread‑safe with concurrent enqueues/dequeues. It should only be used when no other threads are accessing the queue.

member.summary: Copies the queue elements to an array.

member.name: T:Nomad.Core.CVars.ICVar`1
member.summary:
A typed generic CVar interface. Used for referencing CVars in code as seen in the examples provided below.
A CVar's type can only be one of the following:
Int32 A 32 bit signed integer value.
UInt32 A 32 bit unsigned integer value.
Boolean An 8 bit boolean true-false value.
Decimal A 32 bit floating-point value.
String A null-terminated string value.
member.typeparam: The CVar's internal type is limited to the types listed in the 's documentation.

member.name: P:Nomad.Core.CVars.ICVar`1.Value
member.summary: The CVar's current internal value. This can only hold values within the type restrictions of , and whenever this variable changes, the event is published.

member.name: P:Nomad.Core.CVars.ICVar`1.DefaultValue
member.summary: The value that the CVar was created with. This cannot change.

member.name: P:Nomad.Core.CVars.ICVar`1.ValueChanged
member.summary: Event that triggers whenever changes. Contains the new value and the previous value.

member.name: T:Nomad.Core.Events.IGameEvent
member.summary: Represents the base type for all game events.
member.remarks: Provides identifying metadata and manual subscription cleanup. Typically inherited by generic game events that carry an event payload.

member.name: P:Nomad.Core.Events.IGameEvent.DebugName
member.summary: Gets the debugger-friendly display name for this event.

member.name: P:Nomad.Core.Events.IGameEvent.NameSpace
member.summary: Gets the logical namespace/category associated with this event.

member.name: P:Nomad.Core.Events.IGameEvent.Id
member.summary: Gets the unique identifier or hash code for this event.

members.member:
Represents a typed game event capable of publishing payload data to subscribers.
The struct payload type used when publishing the event.
Subscriptions are tied to an owner object. Subscriptions automatically clean themselves up when the owner goes out of scope or is destroyed.
# Examples
## Godot Here's an example of how to subscribe to an IGameEvent in Godot: \include Nomad.Events/Godot/Player_Subscribe.cs
## Unity ### Declaring an event variable \snippet Nomad.Events/Unity/Player_Subscribe.cs declare ### Retrieving an event \snippet Nomad.Events/Unity/Player_Subscribe.cs get_event ### Subscribing to an event \snippet Nomad.Events/Unity/Player_Subscribe.cs subscribe ### Publishing an event \snippet Nomad.Events/Unity/Enemy_Publish.cs publish This will allow you to send messages between scripts without ever directly calling into them.
@module Nomad.Events @stability Stable @since 1.0.0 @version 1.0.0

member.name: F:Nomad.Core.Input.GamepadButton.A
member.summary: Game controller SDL button A. Corresponds to the bottom action button: Sony Cross, Xbox A, Nintendo B.

member.name: F:Nomad.Core.Input.GamepadButton.B
member.summary: Game controller SDL button B. Corresponds to the right action button: Sony Circle, Xbox B, Nintendo A.

member.name: F:Nomad.Core.Input.GamepadButton.X
member.summary: Game controller SDL button X. Corresponds to the left action button: Sony Square, Xbox X, Nintendo Y.

member.name: F:Nomad.Core.Input.GamepadButton.Y
member.summary: Game controller SDL button Y. Corresponds to the top action button: Sony Triangle, Xbox Y, Nintendo X.

member.name: F:Nomad.Core.Input.GamepadButton.Back
member.summary: Game controller SDL back button. Corresponds to the Sony Select, Xbox Back, Nintendo - button.

member.name: F:Nomad.Core.Input.GamepadButton.Guide

member.name: T:Nomad.Core.Logger.ILoggerCategory
member.summary: Represents a logging category.
member.remarks: Categories show which part of a module/which system the log message is coming from to make tracing messages much easier instead of guesswork.

member.name: P:Nomad.Core.Logger.ILoggerCategory.Name
member.summary: The name of the category.

member.name: P:Nomad.Core.Logger.ILoggerCategory.Level
member.summary: The log level of the category.

member.name: P:Nomad.Core.Logger.ILoggerCategory.Enabled
member.summary: Whether the category is enabled. false means all messages sent with this category as a parameter will simply be ignored.

member.name: M:Nomad.Core.Logger.ILoggerCategory.PrintLine(System.String)
member.summary: Prints a message to this category.

member.name: M:Nomad.Core.Logger.ILoggerCategory.AddSink(Nomad.Core.Logger.ILoggerSink)
member.summary: Adds a sink to the category.
member.remarks: By default, all sinks are added to a category, however, if you wish to control where a category can actually output to, utilize this method here to do so.
member.param: The sink to add.

member.name: M:Nomad.Core.Logger.ILoggerCategory.RemoveSink(Nomad.Core.Logger.ILoggerSink)
member.summary: Removes a sink from the category.
member.remarks: By default, all sinks are added to a category, however, if you wish to control where a category can actually output to, utilize this method here to do so.
member.param: The sink to remove.

member.summary: The generic logger interface. Controls sink and category management.

member.summary: Prints a log message with a newline.

member.summary: Prints a debug log message.

member.summary: Prints a warning log message.

member.summary: Prints an error log message.

member.param: The message to print.

member.name: M:Nomad.Core.Logger.ILoggerService.Clear
member.summary: Clears all logged messages (in all categories, clears all sinks). This will reset all messages. This action in irreversible.

member.name: M:Nomad.Core.Logger.ILoggerService.InitConfig(Nomad.Core.CVars.ICVarSystemService)
member.summary: Initializes the logger configuration.
member.param: The CVar system.

member.name: M:Nomad.Core.Logger.ILoggerService.AddSink(Nomad.Core.Logger.ILoggerSink)
member.summary: Adds a sink to the logger.
member.remarks: A sink is a dedicated output abstraction, that prints messages to IO outputs such as stdout, a logging file, or the in-game console. You can setup your own sink if you want to route log messages elswhere.
member.param: The sink to add.

member.name: M:Nomad.Core.Logger.ILoggerService.CreateCategory(System.String,Nomad.Core.Logger.LogLevel,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Creates a new logging category.
member.remarks: A logging category is a denotation of where a message is coming from for easier debugging and tracing. You can also filter categories by enabling or disabling them as well as by .
member.param: The name of the category.
The log level of the category.
Whether the category is enabled. If false, any log messages sent with this category as a parameter will not send any messages to the sinks.
member.returns: The created logging category.

member.name: T:Nomad.Core.Util.Any
member.summary: A type-safe container that can hold any of the supported primitive types. Similar to C++'s std::any, it provides a unified way to store and retrieve different value types in a memory-efficient manner using a union structure.

remarks.para: The struct occupies exactly 20 bytes of memory regardless of the stored type, using an explicit layout union to share the same memory space.
This type is particularly useful in scenarios where type erasure is needed while maintaining type safety through explicit conversion or retrieval.

member.name: P:Nomad.Core.Util.Any.Type
member.summary: The internal system type.

member.param: The boolean value to store.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the struct with a signed 8-bit integer.
member.param: The signed 8-bit integer value to store.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the struct with a signed 16-bit integer.
member.param: The signed 16-bit integer value to store.

member.name: T:Nomad.Core.Util.InternString
member.summary: Represents an interned string, providing efficient storage and comparison of string values.

remarks.para:
An is a lightweight value type that stores an integer identifier pointing to a unique string instance in the global string pool. This enables:
Reduced memory usage for duplicate strings
Fast equality comparisons (integer comparison instead of string comparison)
Efficient hashing (the identifier itself serves as the hash code)
Interned strings are managed by the class, which ensures that identical strings share the same storage and identifier.
This struct implements for efficient equality comparisons without boxing. See the remarks on the method for details.

member.name: F:Nomad.Core.Util.InternString.Empty
member.summary: Gets an empty instance.
member.value: An instance representing an empty string.
member.remarks: The empty has an identifier of 0, which is reserved for the empty string in the string pool.

member.name: M:Nomad.Core.Util.InternString.#ctor(System.UInt64)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the struct with a specific identifier.
member.param: The identifier of the interned string in the string pool.

remarks.para: This constructor is typically used internally by the string interning system. For user code, prefer the constructor that takes a of characters or use implicit conversion from strings.
Note: This constructor does not validate that the identifier corresponds to an existing interned string. Using an invalid identifier may result in undefined behavior when the string is accessed.

member.name: M:Nomad.Core.Util.InternString.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the struct from a character span.
member.param: The character span containing the string value to intern.

remarks.para: This constructor interns the specified string value in the global string pool. If an identical string is already interned, the existing identifier is reused.
The interning process may allocate memory in the string pool if the string is not already present.

member.name: M:Nomad.Core.Util.InternString.Equals(Nomad.Core.Util.InternString)
member.summary: Determines whether this instance and another specified are equal.
member.param: The to compare with this instance.
member.returns: if has the same identifier as this instance; otherwise, .

remarks.para: This method provides an efficient equality comparison by comparing only the integer identifiers, avoiding string comparison entirely.
This method is aggressively inlined for performance.
Implementing prevents boxing when comparing instances in generic contexts or collections that use the interface.

member.name: M:Nomad.Core.Util.InternString.Equals(System.Object)
member.summary: Determines whether this instance and a specified object are equal.
member.param: The object to compare with this instance.
member.returns: if is an and has the same identifier as this instance; otherwise, .
member.remarks: This method is aggressively inlined for performance.

member.name: M:Nomad.Core.Util.InternString.GetHashCode
member.summary: Returns the hash code for this instance.
member.returns: The identifier of this interned string, which serves as its hash code.

remarks.para:
The hash code is simply the integer identifier, which provides:
Perfect distribution (each interned string has a unique identifier)
Consistency with equality (equal instances have the same hash code)
Minimal computation (the identifier is already available)
This method is aggressively inlined for performance.

member.name: M:Nomad.Core.Util.InternString.ToString
member.summary: Converts the interned string to a .

member.name: M:Nomad.Core.Util.InternString.op_Implicit(Nomad.Core.Util.InternString)~System.String
member.summary: Converts an to a regular string.
member.param: The to convert.
member.returns: The string value represented by the , or if the identifier is invalid or represents an empty string.

remarks.para: This conversion looks up the string value in the string pool using the identifier. If the identifier is invalid (does not correspond to an interned string), is returned.
This method is aggressively inlined for performance, though the underlying string pool lookup may involve a dictionary lookup.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.IsExternalInit
member.summary: According to the principal lead developer on the C# Language Team, Jared Parsons, this is not included in any version of the framework older than .NET v5, hence it must be defined manually here when we compile for unity.
member.remarks: From stackoverflow post https://stackoverflow.com/questions/64749385/predefined-type-system-runtime-compilerservices-isexternalinit-is-not-defined
